title: Viral metagenomics using SMART-9n amplification and nanopore sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fitcl","text":"This is a SMART-Seq (Switch Mechanism at the 5′ End of RNA Templates) protocol developed to use random 9n priming and be compatible with ONT RLB rapid, barcoded adapters available from the SQK-RPB004 kit. We have generated cDNA reads in excess of 10 kb from Zika viral reference material with this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aj6ak","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Transfer up to 500 µL sample directly onto a Ultrafree-MC Centrifugal Filter column
Spin at 5000 x g for 00:01:00
Recover filtrate into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube
Remove basket and discard
Close lid and place on ice
In a 2 mL Eppendorf tube combine 200 µL sample, 200 µL DNA/RNA Shield (2x concentrate) and mix well by pipetting
Add 800 µL Viral RNA Buffer and mix well by pipetting
Load 600 µL onto a column in a collection tube and spin at 10000 x g for 00:00:15 discard flow through, place in a new collection tube
Add 500 µL Viral wash buffer and spin at 10000 x g for 00:00:15 , discard flow through
Add 500 µL 100% ethanol and spin at 10000 x g for 00:01:00 , discard flow through, place in a clean 1.5 mL tube
Add 15 µL of DNA/RNA-Free Water and incubate at RT for 00:03:00
Spin at 10000 x g for 00:00:15
Set heat block to 37 °C
Set up the following reaction: Component Volume RNA 44 µL 10X TURBO DNase Buffer 5 µL TURBO DNase 1 µL Total 50 µL
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00
Add 100 µL RNA Binding Buffer and mix by vortexing 00:00:05 and spin down
Add 150 µL 100% ethanol and mix by vortexing for 00:00:15 and spin down
Transfer 300 µL to a Zymo-Spin IC column in a 2 mL collection tube and spin at 6000 x g for 00:00:15 discard flow through
Add 400 µL RNA Prep Buffer and spin at 6000 x g for 00:00:15 , discard flow through
Add 700 µL RNA Wash Buffer and spin at 6000 x g for 00:00:15 , discard flow through and place in a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube
Add 10 µL DNase/RNase Free Water and incubate at RT for 00:01:00
Spin at 6000 x g for 00:00:15
Label as ‘Viral RNA’ and place on ice
Set heat block to 55 °C
In a 2 mL Eppendorf tube combine 200 µL sample, 200 µL DNA/RNA Shield (2X concentrate), 20 µL Proteinase K and mix well by pipetting
Incubate at 55 °C for 00:30:00
Set heat block to 60 °C
Add 1.200 mL binding buffer and mix well
Load 800 µL onto a Zymo-Spin IIC-Z column in a collection tube and spin at 8000 x g for 00:00:15 , discard the discard flow-through and reload as many times as required
Transfer to to a new collection tube add 400 µL DNA Wash Buffer 1 and spin at 8000 x g for 00:00:15 , discard flow-through
Add 700 µL DNA Wash Buffer 2 and spin at 8000 x g for 00:00:15 .
Add 200 µL DNA Wash Buffer 2 and spin at 8000 x g for 00:01:00 .
Transfer to a new 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube and 50 µL DNA/RNA Free Water preheated to 60 °C to the column, incubate at RT for 00:01:00 .
Spin at 8000 x g for 00:01:00 .
Combine the following in a 0.2ml 8-strip tube. Component Volume RLB RT 9N (2 uM) 1 µL dNTPs (10 mM ea.) 1 µL Template RNA 10 µL Total 12 µL
Mix and spin down.
Incubate at 65 °C for 00:05:00 then snap cool on ice.

Make up the following master mix and add 8 µL to the 12 µL annealed RNA: Component Volume SSIV buffer (5x) 4 µL DTT (100 mM) 1 µL RNase OUT 1 µL SS IV RTase (200 U/ul) 1 µL RLB TSO (2 uM) 1 µL Total 20 µL
Start the following program on a thermocycler: 42 °C for 01:30:00 70 °C for 00:10:00
Set up the following reaction to amplify cDNA in a new 0.2ml 8-strip tube: Component Volume LongAmp Taq 2X master mix 25 µL RLB (01-12) 0.5 µL NFW 19.5 µL cDNA 5 µL Total 50 µL

Start the following program on the thermal cycler: Step Temperature Time Cycles Heat Activation 95 °C 00:00:45 1 Denaturation 95 °C 00:00:15 26 Annealing 56 °C 00:00:15 26 Extension 65 °C 00:05:00 26 Final extension 65 °C 00:10:00 1 Hold 4 °C Indefinite 1
Clean up products with 1x Ampure XP and elute in 30 ul EB Amplicon clean-up using SPRI beads by Josh Quick
Quantify the PCR products using the Quantus Fluorometer using the ONE dsDNA assay. DNA quantification using the Quantus fluorometer by Josh Quick
Pool all barcoded products to a total of 200 Femtomolar (fM) in 10 µL of 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl pH 8.0 with 50 millimolar (mM) NaCl
Add 1 µL RAP adapter and mix by pipetting, incubate at RT 00:05:00
Prime the flowcell and load sequencing library onto the flowcell. Priming and loading a MinION flowcell by Josh Quick
creator: Josh Quick
